task_completion.description: Open or read the input FITS files for [SII] and H-alpha.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the agent opening or reading '673nmos.fits' and '656nmos.fits' (e.g., using astropy.io.fits.open in a Python script, or loading them into an astronomical image viewer).

task_completion.description: Divide the [SII] image by the H-alpha image and ensure 32-bit float format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of a division operation where the [SII] data (673nmos) is the numerator and H-alpha (656nmos) is the denominator, along with casting or saving the result as a 32-bit float (e.g., 'astype(np.float32)' in Python).

task_completion.description: Save the resulting ratio map as a new FITS file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the resulting FITS file being saved to '~/AstroImages/eagle_ratio/sii_ha_ratio.fits' (e.g., a 'writeto' command in code or the file appearing in the file explorer/terminal).

task_completion.description: Calculate the minimum, maximum, median, and standard deviation of the ratio map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the agent computing the minimum, maximum, median, and standard deviation of the ratio array (e.g., using numpy functions like np.min, np.max, np.median, np.std).

task_completion.description: Save the calculated statistics to a text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of writing the four calculated statistics to '~/AstroImages/eagle_ratio/ratio_statistics.txt' (e.g., Python file write operations, terminal echo commands, or the text file open in an editor showing the values).

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually computed the statistics from the image data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent runs a script or uses a tool to process the FITS files and extract statistics. It should not just create a text file with hallucinated or hardcoded numbers.

integrity.description: Verify that the division operation was performed in the correct order.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the math explicitly divides 673nmos ([SII]) by 656nmos (H-alpha). If 656nmos is divided by 673nmos, this is a failure.

integrity.description: Verify that the output FITS file is a genuine data file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent does not simply use 'touch sii_ha_ratio.fits' or an equivalent command to create an empty file just to bypass the FITS generation requirement.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses real Hubble Space Telescope data of the Eagle Nebula (M16). The H-alpha data is in 656nmos.fits and the [SII] data is in 673nmos.fits. The expected outputs are a FITS file at /home/ga/AstroImages/eagle_ratio/sii_ha_ratio.fits and a text file at /home/ga/AstroImages/eagle_ratio/ratio_statistics.txt.
